coredata.dc:title: Synergic effect of graphene quantum dots and room temperature ionic liquid for the fabrication of highly sensitive voltammetric sensor for levodopa determination in the presence of serotonin
coredata.dc:creator: Sanati, Afsaneh L.
Faridbod, Farnoush
Ganjali, Mohammad Reza
coredata.dc:description: Levodopa, one of the prescribed medications in Parkinson disease, is electrochemically determined here. A room temperature ionic liquid/graphene quantum dots (GQDs) modified carbon paste electrode (RTIL-GQDs/CPE) was used for the electrochemical determination of levodopa. The electro-oxidation mechanism of levodopa at RTIL-GQDs/CPE was also studied. The obtained data showed that the electro-oxidation of levodopa at the RTIL-GQDs/CPE is greatly facilitated, which is attributed to high electrical conductivity of GQDs and RTIL. The electrochemical oxidation of levodopa at the RTIL-GQDs/CPE is a two proton–two electron process. The RTIL-GQDs/CPE showed a good electron mediating behavior followed by well separated oxidation signals of levodopa and serotonin. Square wave voltammetric peak current shows a linear relationship corresponds to the concentrations of levodopa in a linear range of 0.05 to 250.0μM with detection limit of 10.0nM. The RTIL-GQDs/CPE shows excellent ability to determination of levodopa in real samples.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Levodopa
Serotonin
Graphene quantum dots
Room temperature ionic liquid

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Electrochemical behavior of levodopa at a surface of RTIL-GQDs/CPE

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Plot of Ep vs. pH for RTIL-GQDs/CPE. Inset; cyclic voltammograms of RTIL-GQDs/CPE at various buffered pHs in the presence of 300μM levodopa.

Linear sweep voltammograms of (a) RTIL-GQDs/CPE; (b) RTIL/CPE; (c) GQDs/CPE and (d) CPE in the buffer solution pH=7.0 in the presence of 500μM levodopa. Inset: the current density derived from cyclic voltammograms responses of 500μM levodopa at pH7.0 at the surface of different electrodes.

Variation of anodic peak current vs. v 1/2 for electro-oxidation of 700μM levodopa. Inset; cyclic voltammograms of RTIL-GQDs/CPE in 0.1mol·L−1 PBS (pH7.0) containing 700μM levodopa at various scan rates; a-h correspond to 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 18.0, 25.0, 40.0, 80.0 and 90.0mV·s−1, respectively.
Tafel plot for RTIL-GQDs/CPE in 0.1M PBS (pH7.0) in the presence of 700μM levodopa and scan rate 10 mV·s−1.
A) Chronoamperograms obtained at the RTIL-GQDs/CPE in the presence of (a) 300 and (b) 500μM levodopa at pH7.0. B) Plots of I vs. t−1/2 obtained from chronoamperograms.

A) Square wave voltammograms of RTIL-GQDs/CPE in pH7.0 containing different concentrations of levodopa and serotonin (from inner to outer) mixed solutions of: (a): 3.0.0+3.0; (b): 10.0+20.0; (c): 20.0+40.0; (d): 35.0+65.0; (e) 50.0+95.0 and (f) 55.0+1100.0μM levodopa and serotonin, respectively. (B) plot of the peak currents as a function of levodopa concentration and (C) plot of the peak currents as a function of serotonin concentration.
Determination of levodopa and serotonin in real samples (number of replicate measurments=5).

head.ce:title: Synergic effect of graphene quantum dots and room temperature ionic liquid for the fabrication of highly sensitive voltammetric sensor for levodopa determination in the presence of serotonin

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Center of Excellence in Electrochemistry, School of Chemistry, College of Science, University of Tehran, Tehran, Iran

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Biosensor Research Center, Endocrinology & Metabolism, Molecular-Cellular Sciences Institute, Tehran University of Medical Sciences, Tehran, Iran

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Levodopa, one of the prescribed medications in Parkinson disease, is electrochemically determined here. A room temperature ionic liquid/graphene quantum dots (GQDs) modified carbon paste electrode (RTIL-GQDs/CPE) was used for the electrochemical determination of levodopa. The electro-oxidation mechanism of levodopa at RTIL-GQDs/CPE was also studied. The obtained data showed that the electro-oxidation of levodopa at the RTIL-GQDs/CPE is greatly facilitated, which is attributed to high electrical conductivity of GQDs and RTIL. The electrochemical oxidation of levodopa at the RTIL-GQDs/CPE is a two proton–two electron process. The RTIL-GQDs/CPE showed a good electron mediating behavior followed by well separated oxidation signals of levodopa and serotonin. Square wave voltammetric peak current shows a linear relationship corresponds to the concentrations of levodopa in a linear range of 0.05 to 250.0μM with detection limit of 10.0nM. The RTIL-GQDs/CPE shows excellent ability to determination of levodopa in real samples.

ce:section.ce:para: Levodopa is an amino acid which plays an important role in humans, some animals and plants. The body is able to make it through biosynthesis from the amino acid L-tyrosine. Levodopa is a precursor of catecholamines neurotransmitters such as dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine. Furthermore, L-DOPA itself mediates neurotrophic factor release by the brain. Levodopa is one of the most important catechol drugs suggested for treatment of Parkinson's disease alone or in combination with Carbidopa [1]. Levodopa can help the movements of the body by improving muscle control [2,3]. Levodopa has shown some side effects in treatment of Parkinson's disease such as hypotension, arrhythmias, nausea, hair loss and disorientation and confusion. Moreover, levodopa directly affects the serotonin systems. Serotonin neurons are capable of taking up levodopa and decarboxylating it to dopamine [4]. Therefore, simultaneous determination of levodopa and serotonin is very important in blood and urine sample in Parkinson's disease. Up to now, some analytical methods have been used for the analysis of L-DOPA, such as high performance chromatography [5], spectrophotometry [6], chemiluminecence [7] and electrochemical sensors [8,9]. However, electrochemical methods can be a superior method of neurotransmitters determination due to the miniaturization of technology and ability of online analysis. Due to this fact, many researchers focused on designing new electrochemical sensors to measure these compounds more selective and sensitive [10–20].

Graphene quantum dots are derivative of graphene nanosheet with unique properties such as high surface area and good electrical conductivity, which can be a good candidate for fabrication of electrochemical sensors [21]. Recently, GQDs, besides their remarkable optical properties, were used as a modifier for increasing electrical conductivity of voltammetric sensors. Shiddiky et al. reported a highly sensitive immunosensor for EpCAM biomarkers using GQDs modified electrode. The proposed sensor showed a 100fg/mL limit of detection for epithelial cell adhesion molecule analysis [22]. Roushani and Abdi reported a novel voltammetric sensor based on GQDs and riboflavin for determination of persulfate with detection limit of 0.2μM [23].

On the other hand, room temperature ionic liquids are highly conductive binders for modification of carbon paste electrodes [24–29]. Base on the previous studies, it seems that coupling of RTIL and GQDs may be useful for fabrication of highly sensitive electrochemical sensor in voltammetric analysis. Hence, in this study, we describe fabrication of a novel voltammetric sensor based on carbon paste electrode modified by synthesized GQDs and 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate (as a binder) for determination of levodopa in the presence of serotonin. The analytical feasibility of RTIL-GQDs/CPE is examined by measuring of levodopa in urine and serum samples.

An Autolab Electrochemical system consisting of a counter electrode (Pt wire), a working electrode (RTIL-GQDs/CPE, 0.284cm2) and a reference electrode (an Ag/AgCl/KClsat) was used for electrochemical experiments. A Transmission electron microscopy (Philips, CM30, 300 Kv) was used for morphological investigation.

Levodopa, serotonin hydrochloride, phosphoric acid, sodium hydroxide, citric acid were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich Company. Graphite powder and paraffin oil were purchased from Merck Company. Levodopa stock solution (0.01M) was prepared by dissolving 0.197g levodopa in 100mL double-distilled water. Serotonin solution (0.01M) was prepared by weighing 0.212g of serotonin hydrochloride to 100.00mL of double-distilled water.

Pharmaceutical serum was purchased from local market (Iranian Blood Transfusion Organization) and was used for real sample determination without any pretreatment except protein lysis by acid. Urine samples were collected from a safe woman and stored in refrigerator. Five milliliters of the urine was centrifuged for 35min at 400rpm was filtered out using a 0.45μm filter. The sample diluted 50 times and was used for real sample analysis by standard addition method.

RTIL-GQDs/CPE was prepared by mixing of 0.18g of 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluoro phosphate, 0.82g of paraffin, 0.07g of GQDs, and 0.93g of graphite powder. In continuous, previous mixture was mixed about 1h and a portion of the paste was filled into end of glass tube which is in contact with a copper wire as a conductive matter.

Citric acid was used as a carbon source for the synthesis of GQDs through pyrolysis method. For this goal, suitable amount of citric acid (2g) was weighed and transferred in to a beaker and was heated at 200°C for a few minutes until citric acid converts to a liquid phase. The heating continued for 30min more until the solution color changed from colorless to orange which indicated GQD was synthesized.

For investigation of GQDs morphology we used TEM method as a powerful technique in characterization of nanomaterial. As can be seen in Fig. 1 , GQDs were synthesized in sheet shape and in the size ~3–5nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Electrochemical behavior of levodopa at a surface of RTIL-GQDs/CPE
ce:section.ce:para: Active surface area of RTIL-GQDs/CPE, RTIL/CPE, GQDs/CPE and CPE were determined by standard solution of 1.0mM·[Fe(CN)6]−3/−4 in the presence of 0.1M KCl by Randles–Sevcik equation and cyclic voltammograms signals. They were 0.22, 0.20, 0.18 and 0.1256cm2 for RTIL-GQDs/CPE, RTIL/CPE, GQDs/CPE and CPE, respectively. The obtained data confirmed highly electrical conductivity of GQDs and RTIL.

The linear sweep voltammograms of 500μM levodopa at all four different electrodes are given in Fig. 3 . As can be seen in Fig. 3 the moving from unmodified carbon paste electrode (curve d) to RTIL-GQDs/CPE (curve a), the oxidation peak current of levodopa increases from 8.84μA to 42.1μA. On the other hand, oxidation peak potential for levodopa reduces from 460mV (for carbon paste electrode) to 400mV (for RTIL-GQDs/CPE). On the other hand, current density data for all of the four electrodes has been presented in Fig. 3 inset. These data confirm electrical conductivity of two mediators and facile electro-oxidation of levodopa at a surface of RTIL-GQDs/CPE.

In the next step, we studied the effect of scan rate on the oxidation peak current of levodopa (Fig. 4 inset). The obtained data confirmed that the anodic peak potential shifted to more positive value for levodopa oxidation with increasing scan rates and also peak current has a linear relation with ν1/2 that suggest diffusion control for electro-oxidation of levodopa (Fig. 4).

Tafel plot (for oxidation of 300μM levodopa at a surface of RTIL-GQDs/CPE) was used for calculation of charge transfer coefficient (Fig. 5 ). As we know, the slope of the Tafel plot is equal to 2.3RT/n(1−α)F which comes up to 0.2149Vdecade−1. We obtained α as 0.86.

Electro-oxidation of 300μM and 500μM levodopa at the RTIL-GQDs/CPE was investigated by employing chronoamperometry for the determination of the diffusion coefficient (D) (Fig. 6 A). The plot of current (I) vs. t−1/2 was showed a linear relationship (obtained from chronoamperometry data) (Fig. 6 B). According to the Cottrell equation and obtained slops from Fig. 6B, the diffusion coefficient could be estimated 2.36×10−5 cm2/s.

Validation of the RTIL-GQDs/CPE for determination of standard levodopa and serotonin was examined via evaluation of limit of detection (LOD), linear dynamic range (LDR) and selectivity. For levodopa, a plot of peak current vs. potential was linear in the concentration range of 0.05–250μM with the LOD (S/N=3) of 10nM while the linear dynamic range obtained for serotonin was 0.5–500μM with limit of detection of 0.1μM.

Application of RTIL-GQDs/CPE for simultaneous determination of levodopa and serotonin was checked by simultaneous concentration changing of two compounds at an optimum condition. Two separated square wave voltammograms were obtained at potential ~400mV and 720mV relative to levodopa and serotonin, respectively that is enough for simultaneous determination of two compounds in mixture samples (Fig. 7A). As can be seen in Fig. 7 B&C, the sensitivity for levodopa or serotonin analysis in the presence of other compound is very similar to sensitivity of them in dynamic range investigation. This point confirmed simultaneous determination of levodopa and serotonin can be done at a surface of RTIL-GQDs/CPE without any interference.

Stability of the RTIL-GQDs/CPE was tested by keeping the proposed sensor at best pH value for 20days and then the square wave voltammograms of 10μM+20μM levodopa and serotonin were recorded and compared with their signals at a first day. 1.2% decrease in levodopa and serotonin signal after 20days was observed which confirmed good stability of RTIL-GQDs/CPE. Also four different RTIL-GQDs/CPEs were fabricated with same procedure for investigation of reproducibility. RSD for the RTIL-GQDs/CPEs signals in the presence of 10μM+20μM levodopa and serotonin for seven analysis was calculated to be 3.5% that confirmed high reproducibility in electrode fabrication.

Next, the interference of some biological or metal ions compounds on oxidation signal of 10.0μM levodopa was checked (error less than ±5.0% is acceptable). 1000-fold of glucose, methionine, alanine, ascorbic acid (after addition of ascorbic oxidase), Na+, Cl−, NO3 − and 300-fold of uric acid had no effect on the levodopa analysis.

For investigation the performance of the RTIL-GQDs/CPE in the complex matrix determination of levodopa in urine and serum samples was done through standard addition method. The obtained data were shown in Table 1 . As can be seen, the RTIL-GQDs/CPE has a good quality for analysis of two compounds in the real samples.

A highly sensitive and good selective electrochemical sensor based on GQDs and RTIL was fabricated for analysis of levodopa in the presence of serotonin. Combination of GQDs and RTIL showed unique properties for modification of carbon paste electrode. The RTIL-GQDs/CPE successfully resolves the overlapped oxidation peaks of levodopa and serotonin by about 320mV. The RTIL-GQDs/CPE showed good ability for analysis of levodopa and serotonin in real samples.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors are grateful to the Research Council of University of Tehran (28805/D024/6) for the financial support of this work.
